hero.title: Easy, type-safe database Access in Fastify servers
hero.description: Query data from MySQL, PostgreSQL & SQL Server databases in Fastify apps with Prisma – a better ORM for JavaScript and TypeScript.

cards.title: Flexible architecture
cards.description: Prisma fits perfectly into your stack, no matter if you're building microservices or monolithic apps.

cards.title: Higher productivity
cards.description: Prisma's gives you autocompletion for database queries, great developer experience and full type safety.

cards.title: Type-safe database client
cards.description: Prisma Client ensures fully type-safe database queries with benefits like autocompletion - even in JavaScript.

cards.title: Intuitive data modeling
cards.description: Prisma's declarative modeling language is simple and lets you intuitively describe your database schema.

cards.title: Easy database migrations
cards.description: Generate predictable and customizable SQL migrations from the declarative Prisma schema.

cards.title: Designed for building APIs
cards.description: Prisma Client reduces boilerplates by providing queries for common API features (e.g. pagination, filters, ...).

how.title: How Prisma and Fastify fit together
how.description: Prisma ORM is a next-generation ORM that's used to query your database in a Fastify server. You can use it as an alternative to writing plain SQL queries, query builders like knex.js or traditional ORMs like TypeORM, MikroORM and Sequelize.

body.content:
REST API

Prisma is used inside your route handlers to read and write data in your database.

prismaPlugin

Fastify allows you to extend its functionality with plugins. You can create a plugin to instantiate and expose the Prisma Client instance to the rest of your application via the Fastify server instance.

GraphQL API

When building GraphQL APIs with Prisma, you use Prisma Client inside your resolver functions to read and write data in your database.

Learn more about Prisma with GraphQL.

Prisma schema

The Prisma schema uses the Prisma schema language to define your database schema. It makes data modeling declarative and intuitive, especially when it comes to modeling relations.

cards.title: Monitoring your GraphQL API with Fastify, Mercurius, and Prisma
cards.description: Exploring some of the practices to ensure the reliable operation of a GraphQL server in addition to helping with production troubleshooting.

cards.title: Fastify REST API starter kit
cards.description: A ready-to-run example project for a REST API with a SQLite database.

cards.title: Fastify GraphQL API starter kit
cards.description: A ready-to-run example project for a GraphQL API with a PostgreSQL database.
